manage_design.when_to_use: Use for design-level lifecycle tasks, not geometry editing.

analyze_design.when_to_use: Use before editing when you need assembly structure, validation, or scene context.
rules: Use returned component_path and body_ref fields from analysis results to target existing components and bodies in later edit calls.
Prefer body_ref.component_path plus body_ref.body over guessing names from the viewport.
For sketch corrections, reuse sketch_ref, curve_ref, constraint_ref, and dimension_ref from get_assembly_tree or validate instead of inventing indices.
Use get_feature_history before editing or deleting a feature so later apply_3d_features actions can target an existing feature by name.

notes: Detects collisions between all components
Captures a right-side orthographic view

create_sketch.when_to_use: Use to create a new sketch on a plane or on a body face before sketch editing or extrusion.
rules: Prefer component_path for nested assemblies.
Use body_name plus face_index when the sketch must be attached to an existing face.
The resolved target component is activated before sketch creation so the sketch lands in the intended assembly context.
If component_path is omitted for nested assemblies, Fusion may use the currently active component and place the sketch in the wrong location.
Always give new sketches semantic names that describe intent or role. Avoid placeholders like Sketch1, Sketch2, or NewSketch.

edit_sketch.when_to_use: Use to modify one existing sketch with multiple 2D operations in a single call.
rules: You can target the sketch with sketch_name or reuse sketch_ref from analyze_design.
For curve edits such as delete_curve, set_construction, or trim, prefer curve_ref from analyze_design and only fall back to raw curve_index when needed.
For constraint and dimension edits, prefer constraint_ref and dimension_ref from analyze_design or validate.
When creating a sketch earlier in the workflow, keep its name semantic and stable so later edit_sketch calls can target it reliably.
Sketch coordinates and distances are passed through in Fusion Design internal units. For lengths, that means centimeters unless you convert before calling.

notes: cx and cy define the rectangle center; x and y define a corner
x1,y1 and x2,y2 define the base edge; x3,y3 defines the third corner side
r is the radius from center to polygon vertices
r is the apothem radius from center to polygon sides
x1,y1 and x2,y2 define one polygon edge
Optional flip switches the polygon to the opposite side of the input edge
Creates a center-to-center slot
x1,y1 and x2,y2 define the centers of the slot end arcs
width uses Fusion Design length units, typically cm
Creates an overall slot
x1,y1 and x2,y2 define the outer slot endpoints
width uses Fusion Design length units, typically cm
Creates a center-point slot
cx,cy is the slot center; x,y defines direction and center-to-end distance
width uses Fusion Design length units, typically cm
Optional height defaults to 1.0 in Fusion length units
Optional font sets the sketch text font name
x and y are sketch offsets in cm
scale defaults to 1.0
Creates a spun profile in the active sketch using Fusion Sketch.createSpunProfileInput
Supports body or selected body face input; face_index or face_indices can target specific faces
Optional bodies can provide multiple source bodies in one call
axis defaults to Z but can also be set with axis_curve_index/axis_curve_ref or axis_body plus axis_edge_index
Optional flags: flip_result, is_axis_projected, is_centerline_added, tolerance
Available in newer Fusion API versions
Uses Fusion Sketch.project2 when available and falls back to the retired project API on older Fusion versions

Supported selectors: current sketch curves via curve_index/curve_indices/curve_ref/curve_refs, or model entities via body plus optional face_index/face_indices, edge_index/edge_indices, vertex_index/vertex_indices
Optional is_linked controls whether projected geometry remains associatively linked to the source
Intersects the specified body with the sketch plane and creates section curves
Includes geometry into the sketch without planar projection
Supported selectors match project_geometry
Projects selected sketch curves onto one or more target faces
Use curve_index/curve_indices/curve_ref/curve_refs to select source sketch curves
Use face_index or face_indices to select target faces on body
projection_type accepts closest_point or along_vector
For along_vector, supply direction_axis, direction_curve_index, or direction_curve_ref
Changes which plane or planar face the sketch is based on
Use plane_name with XY, XZ, or YZ, or use body plus face_index to redefine onto a planar face
Finds end-connected sketch curves starting from the specified seed curve
Useful before offset and chain-based operations
curve_ref.curve_index is also accepted
Use curve_indices/curve_refs to offset an explicit selection, or seed_curve_index/seed_curve_ref to auto-expand a connected chain using findConnectedCurves
curve_ref.curve_index is also accepted
curve_refs with curve_index are also accepted
curve_refs with window selection are also accepted
curve_refs and mirror_curve_ref are also accepted; the mirror axis must be a sketch line
curve_ref.curve_index is also accepted
curve_ref.curve_index is also accepted
Use curve_index or curve_ref for single-curve constraints; use curve_indices or curve_refs for pair constraints
Accepted type aliases: HorizontalConstraint, VerticalConstraint, ParallelConstraint, PerpendicularConstraint, CollinearConstraint, TangentConstraint, ConcentricConstraint
constraint_ref.constraint_index is also accepted
Use curve_index or curve_ref for single-curve dimensions; use curve_indices or curve_refs for pair dimensions
Accepted type aliases: Distance, DistanceDimension, RadialDimension, DiameterDimension, AngularDimension
dimension_ref.dimension_index is also accepted
dimension_ref.dimension_index is also accepted

apply_3d_features.when_to_use: Use for most solid modeling changes after a sketch exists.
rules: Prefer component_path on each operation when working in nested components.
Use target_body for Join/Cut when the target body is known.
Use get_feature_history from analyze_design before edit_feature or delete_feature so the feature name comes from the actual timeline.
Component-aware creation actions activate the resolved target component before creating bodies.
If component_path is omitted for nested assemblies, primitive creation may fall back to the wrong active component.
Always provide semantic body names for new bodies. Avoid placeholders like Body1, Body2, or NewBody.
Length values are passed through in Fusion Design internal units. For distances, that means centimeters unless you convert before calling.
Angle values passed as plain numbers use Fusion internal angle units, which means radians.

notes: Provide depth unless through_all is true
Counterbore uses cb_dia and cb_depth
Countersink uses cs_dia and cs_angle in radians when sent as a plain number
angle is in radians when sent as a plain number
pull_plane defaults to XY
Advanced: centerline_sketch uses a single connected curve/path
Advanced: taper, twist
Angle values are radians when sent as plain numbers
Advanced: axis, axis_sketch, angle
Angle values are radians when sent as plain numbers
axis defaults to Z
total_angle is in radians when sent as a plain number
Optional second direction via count_y and dist_y
axis_one defaults to X and axis_two defaults to Y
The path sketch should contain a single connected curve/path
Supports translation (x, y, z) and rotation (rx, ry, rz in radians) around center of mass
Supports optional rotation (rx, ry, rz in radians) after translation
Expert: Executes arbitrary Python code within a batch operation. Provides access to adsk, app, design, root, active_comp, params, and returnValue list.
Patterns a feature (e.g. Extrude, Hole) instead of a body. axis defaults to Z.
Rectangular pattern for a feature.
Mirrors a feature across a plane (XY, XZ, YZ). plane defaults to XY.
plane_type can be 'offset' (needs base_plane and offset) or 'angle' (needs axis and angle in radians).
axis_type can be 'two_point' (needs x1,y1,z1 and x2,y2,z2) or 'cylinder' (needs body and face_index).
Creates a thread on a cylindrical face. Optional: face_index (default 0), thread_type, size, designation, thread_class, is_modeled.
Creates a coil/helix. Optional: x, y, z (center), op (NewBody/Join/Cut).
property can be 'area' or 'length'. Returns matching indices. Use min_val and max_val to filter.
Aligns 'body' to the normal of 'target_body' at 'face_index'. align_axis specifies which axis of 'body' points along the normal (X, Y, or Z).
Applies a taper angle (in radians) to an existing Extrude feature.

edit_assembly.when_to_use: Use for assembly structure and joints.
rules: Prefer component_path over component_name for nested assemblies.
For joints, prefer component1_path and component2_path.
Create components first, then use their component paths in later sketch or body creation so activation can target the correct assembly context.
After create_component, use the returned or expected component path in later calls instead of relying on the currently active component.
Always give components semantic names that describe function or placement. Avoid placeholders like Component1, Component2, or New Component.

notes: Creates a joint between parts already in position
Enables/disables physical collision simulation

edit_surfaces.when_to_use: Use for grouped surface operations instead of direct solid modeling tools.
rules: Prefer component_path when the action references a sketch in a nested component.
Use body_names for stitch and body for offset or thicken.
Provide semantic names for newly created surface bodies whenever possible.

edit_forms.when_to_use: Use for grouped T-Spline form operations.
rules: Use sketch plus dist for form extrusion.
Use body for display mode, symmetry, or conversion actions on existing T-Spline bodies.
Provide semantic names for newly created form bodies whenever possible.

import_mesh.when_to_use: Use to bring STL or OBJ geometry into the active Fusion design.

edit_mesh.when_to_use: Use for grouped mesh repair, smoothing, remesh, or conversion actions.
rules: Each action requires body and the target must already be a mesh body.
Use density for remesh, smoothing for smooth, repair_type for repair, and conv_type for convert.

list_parameters.when_to_use: Use before editing parameters when you need current parameter names or values.

edit_parameters.when_to_use: Use to create, update, or delete user parameters in one call.

export_model.when_to_use: Use only after the design is in the desired final state.

execute_python_script.when_to_use: Expert: Use to execute complex modeling logic directly in Fusion 360 using the Python API. Best for loops, mathematical patterns, or operations not exposed via standard tools.
rules: The script has access to 'adsk', 'app', 'design', 'root', 'active_comp', and 'params'.
Store results to be returned in the 'returnValue' list (e.g. returnValue.append('Success')).
Use this tool sparingly and prefer standard tools when possible for better traceability.
